title: Chickpea and green-banana flour are the active microbiota-repairing ingredients in MDCF formulations
abstract: Microbiome-directed complementary foods (MDCFs) repair immature gut microbiota in undernourished children, but which ingredients carry the effect has been unknown. In a four-arm randomized factorial trial we deconstructed an MDCF into its components and fed single-ingredient prototypes to children with moderate acute malnutrition. Chickpea and green-banana flour each independently expanded growth-associated taxa (Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, Prevotella copri) and lifted growth-associated plasma proteins, whereas rice and oil arms did not. The combination was additive. These results identify the specific complementary-food ingredients that most effectively repair an immature microbiota and restore growth.
